title: Gentle Creamy Brazilian Black Bean Stew
description: A funky and tender lacto-vegetarian Brazilian dish featuring creme fraiche and ferment, perfect for a crowd, this stew is a delicious and satisfying meal option.
instructions: In a large pot, saute the chopped onion and minced garlic in a little bit of oil until softened.
Add the soaked and drained black beans, vegetable broth, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and let simmer for 30 minutes.
Use an immersion blender to puree the stew to the desired consistency.
Stir in the creme fraiche and fermented hot sauce, if using.
Let the stew simmer for an additional 10 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs or scallions, if desired.
